Operation 'cloudresourcemanager.organizations.setIamPolicy' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity on return value (Policy object). Added explicit mention of what is returned to help AI agents understand the operation's output and side effects.

data.current_description: Sets the access control policy on an Organization resource. Replaces any existing policy. The `resource` field should be the organization's resource name, e.g. "organizations/123". Authorization requires the Google IAM permission `resourcemanager.organizations.setIamPolicy` on the specified organization
data.summary_suggestion: Set IAM access control policy on organization
data.description_suggestion: Sets the access control policy on an Organization resource, replacing any existing policy. The `resource` field should be the organization's resource name, e.g. "organizations/123". Requires the `resourcemanager.organizations.setIamPolicy` permission. Returns the updated Policy object containing the new access control bindings.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity on return value (Policy object). Added explicit mention of what is returned to help AI agents understand the operation's output and side effects.

message: Operation 'cloudresourcemanager.organizations.setOrgPolicy' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Missing summary entirely. Description uses generic 'Policy' terminology without clarifying it refers to OrgPolicy (Organization Policy). Missing resource name format example, input details about etag behavior, and output information. Added domain-specific terminology consistency and clearer explanation of conditional vs unconditional updates for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'cloudresourcemanager.projects.setIamPolicy' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Missing summary entirely. Description is extremely verbose and difficult for AI agents to parse. Added concise summary and condensed description while preserving critical constraints and warnings. Clarified that it returns the updated Policy object and reorganized constraints for better readability and AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Sets the IAM access control policy for the specified Project. CAUTION: This method will replace the existing policy, and cannot be used to append additional IAM settings. NOTE: Removing service accounts from policies or changing their roles can render services completely inoperable. It is important to understand how the service account is being used before removing or updating its roles. For additional information about `resource` (e.g. my-project-id) structure and identification, see [Resource Names](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/resource_names). The following constraints apply when using `setIamPolicy()`: + Project does not support `allUsers` and `allAuthenticatedUsers` as `members` in a `Binding` of a `Policy`. + The owner role can be granted to a `user`, `serviceAccount`, or a group that is part of an organization. For example, group@myownpersonaldomain.com could be added as an owner to a project in the myownpersonaldomain.com organization, but not the examplepetstore.com organization. + Service accounts can be made owners of a project directly without any restrictions. However, to be added as an owner, a user must be invited via Cloud Platform console and must accept the invitation. + A user cannot be granted the owner role using `setIamPolicy()`. The user must be granted the owner role using the Cloud Platform Console and must explicitly accept the invitation. + You can only grant ownership of a project to a member by using the Google Cloud console. Inviting a member will deliver an invitation email that they must accept. An invitation email is not generated if you are granting a role other than owner, or if both the member you are inviting and the project are part of your organization. + If the project is not part of an organization, there must be at least one owner who has accepted the Terms of Service (ToS) agreement in the policy. Calling `setIamPolicy()` to remove the last ToS-accepted owner from the policy will fail. This restriction also applies to legacy projects that no longer have owners who have accepted the ToS. Edits to IAM policies will be rejected until the lack of a ToS-accepting owner is rectified. If the project is part of an organization, you can remove all owners, potentially making the organization inaccessible. Authorization requires the Google IAM permission `resourcemanager.projects.setIamPolicy` on the project
data.summary_suggestion: Replace IAM access control policy for project
data.description_suggestion: Sets the IAM access control policy for the specified Project, replacing the existing policy entirely. CAUTION: This method replaces the entire policy and cannot be used to append additional IAM settings. NOTE: Removing service accounts from policies or changing their roles can render services completely inoperable. It is important to understand how the service account is being used before removing or updating its roles. Returns the updated Policy object. For additional information about `resource` (e.g. my-project-id) structure and identification, see [Resource Names](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/resource_names). The following constraints apply: Projects do not support `allUsers` and `allAuthenticatedUsers` as members; owner role can only be granted to users, service accounts, or organization groups; at least one ToS-accepting owner must remain; removing all owners in an organization may make it inaccessible. Authorization requires the Google IAM permission `resourcemanager.projects.setIamPolicy` on the project.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is extremely verbose and difficult for AI agents to parse. Added concise summary and condensed description while preserving critical constraints and warnings. Clarified that it returns the updated Policy object and reorganized constraints for better readability and AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Lists Projects that the caller has the `resourcemanager.projects.get` permission on and satisfy the specified filter. This method returns Projects in an unspecified order. This method is eventually consistent with project mutations; this means that a newly created project may not appear in the results or recent updates to an existing project may not be reflected in the results. To retrieve the latest state of a project, use the GetProject method. NOTE: If the request filter contains a `parent.type` and `parent.id` and the caller has the `resourcemanager.projects.list` permission on the parent, the results will be drawn from an alternate index which provides more consistent results. In future versions of this API, this List method will be split into List and Search to properly capture the behavioral difference.
data.summary_suggestion: List Projects accessible to the caller
data.description_suggestion: Lists Projects that the caller has `resourcemanager.projects.get` permission on and match the specified filter criteria. Returns Projects in unspecified order with eventual consistency semantics—newly created or recently updated Projects may not appear immediately. For current state, use GetProject. When filter includes parent.type and parent.id with `resourcemanager.projects.list` permission on parent, results use a more consistent index. Returns paginated list of Project objects.

message: Operation 'cloudresourcemanager.projects.create' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Missing summary entirely. data.current_description: Request that a new Project be created. The result is an Operation which can be used to track the creation process. This process usually takes a few seconds, but can sometimes take much longer. The tracking Operation is automatically deleted after a few hours, so there is no need to call DeleteOperation. Authorization requires the Google IAM permission `resourcemanager.projects.create` on the specified parent for the new project. The parent is identified by a specified ResourceId, which must include both an ID and a type, such as organization. This method does not associate the new project with a billing account. You can set or update the billing account associated with a project using the [`projects.updateBillingInfo`] (/billing/reference/rest/v1/projects/updateBillingInfo) method.
data.summary_suggestion: Request creation of a new Project
data.description_suggestion: Requests creation of a new Project under the specified parent resource. Returns a long-running Operation that tracks the creation process (typically completes in seconds but may take longer). Requires `resourcemanager.projects.create` permission on the parent. Parent must be specified as a ResourceId with both type and ID (e.g., organization). Note: This operation does not associate a billing account; use projects.updateBillingInfo to configure billing separately. The tracking Operation is automatically deleted after a few hours.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is verbose and includes unnecessary details about Operation cleanup. Reorganized to prioritize: (1) what is created, (2) return type (long-running Operation), (3) permissions, (4) parent requirements, (5) billing caveat. Improved clarity for AI agents to understand this is an async operation.

data.current_description: Marks the Project identified by the specified `project_id` (for example, `my-project-123`) for deletion. This method will only affect the Project if it has a lifecycle state of ACTIVE. This method changes the Project's lifecycle state from ACTIVE to DELETE_REQUESTED. The deletion starts at an unspecified time, at which point the Project is no longer accessible. Until the deletion completes, you can check the lifecycle state checked by retrieving the Project with GetProject, and the Project remains visible to ListProjects. However, you cannot update the project. After the deletion completes, the Project is not retrievable by the GetProject and ListProjects methods. The caller must have delete permissions for this Project.
data.summary_suggestion: Mark a Project for deletion
data.description_suggestion: Marks the specified Project for deletion by changing its lifecycle state from ACTIVE to DELETE_REQUESTED. Only affects Projects in ACTIVE state. Requires delete permissions on the Project. After marking, the Project becomes inaccessible and cannot be updated, though it remains visible in ListProjects until deletion completes. Once deletion completes, the Project is no longer retrievable. Actual deletion begins at an unspecified time and may take several hours.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is verbose with redundant phrasing. Reorganized to clarify: (1) what happens (lifecycle state change), (2) preconditions (ACTIVE state), (3) permissions required, (4) side effects (inaccessible, not updatable), (5) visibility during deletion, (6) final state. Improved clarity about state transitions for AI agent decision-making.
